svg.text: 1. Vehicular approach assumed from the high-station end (derived from side-of-street + route direction). SITE-WALK CONFIRM: verify traffic direction in the adjacent lane; one-way streets can flip this.
2. Frontage window OVERRIDDEN to 35 ft — extends past the business's own storefront. §4.1 requires letters of consent from the adjacent property owner(s) AND ground-floor tenant(s).
3. Fire Department Connection locations are building-mounted and not in any DC dataset — SITE-WALK: confirm FDC position; enclosure needs a 3 ft gap at it.
4. Building entrance locations are not in DC Open Data — SITE-WALK: mark building entrance(s) on this plan before submission (§5.2 site plan checklist).

5. Sidewalk width 23'-10" measured curb-to-façade from DC Building Footprints. DDOT Roadway Block reports 16+ (IB) / 14 (OB) — includes planting zone; IB/OB side mapping unverified. FIELD-VERIFY clear ADA path of travel (5 ft min) on site walk.
6. Existing utilities in/under/adjacent to the streatery are not in DC Open Data — the Utility Access Plan (§5.2 item 5) requires field locating + APWA color-code markings before install.

7. §5.2 requires PHOTOS of existing street trees + tree pits with the site plan. Tree box dimensions from Urban Forestry where reported; dashed boxes are unreported (sentinel/missing) — measure on site walk.

8. METER SCHEDULE — meters on this side of the block (some may sit beyond the drawn window): 14793192 (98'-1 1/2" from structure, governs 10 spaces). Multi-space pay stations govern spaces beyond their pole — CONFIRM which meters/spaces are removed or reprogrammed with DDOT at permit review.
9. Existing curbside regulation (DDOT meter data): 07:00AM-08:30PM Mon-Fri $2.30 120 — verify posted signage on site walk.
10. Seating layout: capacity 11 per §4.8 (176 usable SF / 15); all seating 1 ft clear of barriers/enclosure. Layout drawn on the G2.00 life-safety sheet (M5).

g.text: AUTOMATED DRAFT — generated by streatery pre-screener v3.
Architect review, refinement, and seal required before submission.
